'''Stephen Wild''' (born 26 April 1981) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 2000s and 2010s. 

He played at representative level for [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]], [[England national rugby league team|England]] and [[Lancashire rugby league team|Lancashire]], and at club level for the [[Wigan Warriors]], the [[Huddersfield Giants]], the [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]] and the [[North Wales Crusaders]], as a {{rlp|wg|ce|sr}} or {{rlp|lf}}.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Background==
Wild was born in [[Wigan]], [[Greater Manchester]], England.{{fact|date=February 2020}}

==Playing career==
Wild signed for [[Wigan Warriors]] from local amateurs [[Wigan St Patricks]]. He joined Wigan Warriors' first-team after progressing through the lower ranks at the club, making his début in 2001. He made his first representative appearance in 2002 for [[Lancashire rugby league team|Lancashire]].{{cn|date=October 2020}} He was signed by Huddersfield Giants in 2006 and agreed a new contract keeping him at the club until 2009.

He was used as a {{rlp|lf}} at Huddersfield Giants, but he has previously played in the {{rlp|sr}} and also as a {{rlp|ce}}. He has previously been compared to [[Nathan Hindmarsh]], currently one of the sport's best players in Wild's position.<ref>[https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/6672595.stm Sharp backs Giants duo for Lions] ''[[BBC Sport]]'' retrieved 19 May 2007.</ref>

Wild played for Huddersfield Giants in the [[2006 Challenge Cup]] Final at {{rlp|lf}} against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] but the Huddersfield Giants lost 12–42.
In June 2007 Wild was called up to the [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] squad for the [[Test match (rugby league)|test match]] against [[France national rugby league team|France]],<ref>{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/6744909.stm|title=Eight new faces in Lions squad|publisher=BBC|date=2007-06-12|access-date=2007-06-12}}</ref> having made his début against [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]] in 2004.<ref>{{cite web |title=Farrell family to tackle Australia |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2004/oct/29/rugbyleague.andywilson1 |website=The Guardian |access-date=12 January 2026 |date=29 October 2004}}</ref> He has also represented [[England national rugby league team|England]] against [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]], as well as [[Lancashire rugby league team|Lancashire]] in [[Rugby League War of the Roses|Origin Series]].

After retiring, Wild opened a gym in [[Wigan]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Ex-Wigan Warriors player Stephen Wild discusses why he is rebranding his gym to focus on both physical and mental health |url=https://www.wigantoday.net/sport/rugby-league/ex-wigan-warriors-player-stephen-wild-discusses-why-he-is-rebranding-his-gym-to-focus-on-both-physical-and-mental-health-4034153 |website=Wigan Today |access-date=12 January 2026 |date=21 February 2023}}</ref>
